title: abutilon bussei
intro: Abutilon bussei, also known as the desert holly or bush calotrope, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Abutilon bussei include its potential to help reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Abutilon bussei can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by drying the plant material to make a tincture or powder for later use.
